name: Ognjen Sobajic
education: Master's degree, Computer Software Engineering from University of Calgary; Bachelor's degree, Computer Science from University of Belgrade; High School Diploma, Mathematics and Computer Science from Mathematical Grammar School, Belgrade, Serbia; Elementary School Žarko Zrenjanin
headline: Senior Software Engineer | Technical lead for Engineering Systems | C++ | C# | Release Manager | AI practitioner | Master’s in Software Engineering
about:
I am proactive and results-driven Senior SDE with extensive experience in software engineering, DevOps, and platform architecture, spanning both startup and enterprise environments. Recently I've enjoyed being an AI trainer, learning about it and embracing myself in the field.

I have demonstrated success at Microsoft as part of the Edge/Chromium browser and WebView2 teams, where I led initiatives to enhance media infrastructure, accelerate CI/CD pipelines, and drive security fuzzing and cross-team collaboration.

Highly skilled in C++, C#, Azure DevOps, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), .NET compiler tooling, and performance optimization, I also have experience in Java, Python, SQL, ASP.NET, and security best practices. I am committed to mentoring, technical interviewing, and advancing developer experience, consistently improving developer tooling and enabling high-impact feature delivery.

Passionate about engineering excellence, scalable systems, and fostering a strong team culture I am also a continuous learner, always seeking new challenges and opportunities to innovate.

My role involved collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless integration and user satisfaction. I actively contributed to the development of innovative solutions that improved service reliability and performance.

experiences.title: AI trainer
experiences.jobDescription: Analyzed and triaged GitHub issues and pull requests across trending open-source Rust repoes to enhance LLM performance.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: Providing detailed feedback for an AI tool that is training to write comments on C++, C#, Rust, Java, YAML and Powershell codebases.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Worked on the integration and advancement of video and audio processing capabilities within the Microsoft Edge browser. Contributed to system infrastructure and test coverage with a focus on GPU-related performance and media functionality. Supported team enablement and collaboration by creating tools and facilitating knowledge transfer.
• Enabled execution of GPU-related tests on Edge handling DirectX/VM issues. These tests have never run before.
• Built the foundational version of a media sample application along with necessary supporting infrastructure.
• Developed a git mechanism to efficiently compare Edge and Chromium codebases to streamline cleanup processes.
• Maintained and enhanced security through ownership of media fuzzers.
• Led internal training sessions to support the WebView2 team’s onboarding into the media technology domain.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
DevOps/CI/CD Technical Lead for Engineering Systems, overseeing all WebView2 Azure Pipelines and IaC.
Supported the development, release, and maintenance of the WebView2 platform, enabling integration of web technologies into native applications. Focused on engineering systems, cross-plat, cross-team collaboration, and product reliability. Contributed to organizational growth through hiring, mentorship, and technical leadership.
• Managed and enhanced the WebView2 SDK release process to ensure consistent and timely updates.
• Conducted technical interviews for internal transfers and external full-time candidates.
• Mentored full-time employees on technical skills and professional growth.
• Collaborated across multiple Microsoft teams including Visual Studio, Windows, and Teams, as well as partner groups within Edge.
• Designed, implemented, and maintained WebView2 features such as WinForms component, UWP file
upload/download, and fuzzer-based security testing.
• Improved Microsoft Child Process Debugging Power Tool to filter processes by command line. C++/C#
• Implemented all DevOps security measures for WebView2 team, ensuring secure CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure.
• Designed and implemented automated API compatibility tests between the WebView2 SDK and runtime, ensuring consistent and reliable integration across versions.

experiences.title: Software Developer 2
experiences.jobDescription: Responsible for Azure DevOps implementing and improving many pipelines. Authored few performance improvements: Sped up pipeline 4h -> 40min affecting ~50 devs, Sped up pipeline by introducing incremental linting, Fixed a critical memory leak. Drastically improved a DevEx feature (tunables) – debugging feature of the Intentional platform

experiences.title: C#/.NET Software Analyst and Developer
experiences.jobDescription: Worked on Developer Experience team and internal tools. Led development of C# Roslyn .NET Compiler analyzers for Intentional codebase. Led Hungarian Helper project – internal tool for disambiguating hungarian notation on Intentinal codebase. Mentored number of interns in C# codebase. Conducted ~60 phone interviews and represented the company on career fairs.

experiences.title: C++ compiler team
experiences.jobDescription:
Worked on CppRestSDK library and improved conformance for C libraries in Visual C++ compiler
• Improved performance of JSON parser in C++/REST Library.
• Implemented Connection Keep-Alive for Linux in CppRestSDK.
• Wrote tests for new experimental C++ features generator/co_yield.

experiences.title: .NET/C# Software Developer
experiences.jobDescription: Developed an internal testing tool used by QA, developers and other departments. Applied innovative testing strategy for this tool. Worked both individually and as a part of the team. Developed and maintained SQL upgrade scripts for Oracle and SQL Server. Suggested various architectural and implementation improvements which have been implemented.

experiences.jobDescription: Part-time software developer. I developed various industrial solutions focusing on specific implementation of algorithms required for civil engineering in the area of water and flow management.

recommendationsReceived.name: Steffen Lösch
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Engineering Manager, Software Engineer, Computer Scientist
recommendationsReceived.context: Steffen worked with Ognjen on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
I've worked with Ognjen within in the same engineering team for multiple years. We've shipped various features together for Microsoft Whiteboard, and I've greatly valued working with him. Ognjen is a highly effective engineer, who will persistently make progress and solve any problem you put him on, from porting .NET apps to Android and iOS, to writing custom compiler validations, to tracking down tricky performance problems, the list goes on. 

Ognjen impressed me in particular with his deep knowledge in Compiler when writing custom static analysis code using the .NET Compiler Platform SDK (Roslyn) and he has had very good C++ compiler knowledge. Additionally, Ognjen ensured the quality of our static analysis logic, by seting up test infrastructure, writing many tests, fixing discovered bugs and maintaining the test infrastructure over time.

There are many traits that make Ognjen a highly effective engineer and team lead, the ones that stand out to me the most to me are his ability to deliver consistent impact, his collaboration skills, and his ability to think creatively out of the box. He will be a great asset to any team he's on.

recommendationsReceived.name: Ling zhong
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Principal Group Software Engineer Manager at Microsoft
recommendationsReceived.description: Ognjen Sobajic has played a pivotal role in advancing pipeline infrastructure through multiple initiatives. He has consistently addressed critical issues by implementing urgent fixes to unblock releases and improve signing reliability, as well as onboarding pipelines into ICM for enhanced monitoring and incident response. Ognjen also coordinated with stakeholders to ensure compliance and artifact readiness. Beyond his technical contributions, he actively participated in coaching others through design reviews and validation discussions. In summary, Ognjen has greatly shaped our strategies for reducing overhead and enhancing pipeline robustness, reflecting his deep technical ownership and proactive leadership in pipeline engineering.

recommendationsReceived.name: Nicolas Champagne-Williamson
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Principal Software Engineering Manager at Microsoft
recommendationsReceived.description:
I had the pleasure of managing Ognjen (Ogi) for five years on the Edge browser team at Microsoft, and one quality that consistently stood out was his proactive mindset. Ogi had a remarkable ability to identify what needed to be done—often before anyone asked—and take initiative to drive meaningful improvements across our engineering systems. He was consistently the first to raise his hand to help when new issues came in that needed immediate attention and was able to juggle multiple contexts to deliver solutions quickly.

He was instrumental in leading efforts to improve test coverage, streamline release processes, and ensure our pipelines were secure and compliant. Whether it was jumping on high-priority work like Executive Order compliance, resolving pipeline issues, or generalizing solutions to benefit teams beyond his immediate scope, Ogi consistently delivered with speed and thoughtfulness.

What also made Ogi a standout teammate was his willingness to help others. He was a go-to person for technical guidance, code reviews, and onboarding support. His responsiveness and clarity made him a trusted collaborator, and his contributions often unblocked others and raised the overall quality of our work.

Ogi’s combination of initiative, technical depth, and team-first attitude made a lasting impact. I’m grateful for his contributions and confident he’ll bring the same energy and effectiveness to any team he joins next!

recommendationsReceived.name: Scott Jones
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Principal Software Engineer at Microsoft
recommendationsReceived.context: Scott worked with Ognjen but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: Ognjen and I collaborated together on the Windows App SDK, factoring the WebView2 integration into a proper nuget package dependency.  It was a fairly steep learning curve and a formidable concept space, and Ognjen immersed himself and spun up quickly on the rearchitecture.  He became proficient in the nuget packaging, MSBuild support, and C#/WinRT projection implementation necessary to land the project on time and robustly.  He demonstrated the excellent communication and partnering skills necessary for a successful cross-group collaboration.  Ognjen is an established, experienced engineer, and would be an asset to any organization considering hiring him.

recommendationsReceived.name: Gregg Miskelly
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Principal Software Design Engineer at Microsoft
recommendationsReceived.context: Gregg worked with Ognjen but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: I worked with Ognjen when he added a feature to the Microsoft Child Process Debugging Power Tool, which is a project that my team (the Visual Studio Debugger team) owns. The power toy is a Visual Studio extension to make it easier to debug programs where the interesting code is spread out into multiple processes. Ognjen wanted to be able to filter processes by command line, which was a useful feature, but something my team didn't have the cycles to work on. Ognjen dove into the code with minimal instructions and was able to figure out how to implement what he needed. He persisted through a bunch of access control problems, figured out how to scrape the additional information from the target process, and was able to reverse engineer the configuration state code to flow his new configuration where it needed to go. I was impressed with his willingness to dive into a code base that he didn't own. It was a pleasure working with him. Thanks for contributing!

summary: Ognjen Sobajic is currently a Microsoft professional working within a large, established tech company and actively sharing hands-on learning and engineering experiences. He frequently posts about applied AI and software engineering topics—computer vision, image processing, Azure AI, machine learning, generative AI, natural language processing, and secure coding in C++. His style is concise, technical and learning-oriented: he announces completed courses and learning paths, links resources, posts short technical reflections, and shares code and experimental outputs to illustrate concepts. He demonstrates practical curiosity by implementing small projects (for example a C++ Markov-chain text predictor) to explore fundamentals behind modern models and by contrasting simple approaches with complex LLM architectures. Recurring patterns include continuous upskilling, resource-sharing, hands-on experimentation, and occasional celebration of team milestones (e.g., a GA release), signaling both individual development and collaborative impact. The unique value in his content is the combination of accessible educational updates, concrete code examples, and thoughtful comparisons that help peers understand both foundational techniques and their relationship to state-of-the-art systems.
keywords: Hands-On AI: Image Processing with Python
Microsoft Azure AI Essentials Professional Certificate
